spec_match.rationale: Demo invokes the relevant API surface (WebGPU 'core-features-and-limits') and surfaces the result in the page, demonstrating the spec behaviour the feature ships.

interactive.rationale: There is one interactive control and a script, but the surface is thin — closer to a probe than a full hands-on demo.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Page surfaces multiple failure / unsupported / edge-case branches in the UI, not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Page cites the chromestatus.com/feature/4744775089258496 link and the spec, as required by CLAUDE.md rule 3.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Add a real interactive control surface
openQuestions.detail: The concept page currently reads more like a write-up than a hands-on demo. Add a control that drives the underlying API (or a faithful simulation) and surfaces the live result on the page — buttons, sliders, selects, or a try-it form. CLAUDE.md rule 7 explicitly bans static code cards as concept pages.

summary: 'Compat Gate' is a solid concept page: interactive surface lands, references are in place, and the design system rules hold. The follow-ups in openQuestions are polish, not blockers.
